chapter_context.summary: Depicts the sharp, polished sword of the Lord drawn against all flesh, dancing and prepared for slaughter, and Nebuchadnezzar at the parting of the ways using divination to decide whether to attack Jerusalem or Rabbah.

outline.text: The Drawn and Unsheathed Sword of the Lord Against the South
outline.explanation: This section focuses on The Drawn and Unsheathed Sword of the Lord Against the South (21:1-7), highlighting its theological significance and showing how it develops the central message of Ezekiel.

outline.text: The Song of the Sharp, Polished Sword Prepared for Slaughter
outline.explanation: This section focuses on The Song of the Sharp, Polished Sword Prepared for Slaughter (21:8-17), highlighting its theological significance and showing how it develops the central message of Ezekiel.

outline.text: Nebuchadnezzar at Crossroad Divining to Attack Jerusalem
outline.explanation: This section focuses on Nebuchadnezzar at Crossroad Divining to Attack Jerusalem (21:18-27), highlighting its theological significance and showing how it develops the central message of Ezekiel.

outline.text: Swift and Unbending Judgment Against Ammonites
outline.explanation: This section focuses on Swift and Unbending Judgment Against Ammonites (21:28-32), highlighting its theological significance and showing how it develops the central message of Ezekiel.

key_themes.title: Prophecies Against Judah and Jerusalem
key_themes.description: This chapter forms a core part of Ezekiel's prophecies concerning prophecies against judah and jerusalem, illustrating God's absolute sovereignty in both justice and final restoration.

key_themes.title: The Glory of the Lord
key_themes.description: A central theological theme in Ezekiel, describing the overwhelming majesty of God's presence, its departure due to sin, and its return in future restoration.

devotional.question: Reflecting on the visions, symbolic actions, and warnings of Chapter 21, what does this teach you about God's holiness and His desire for deep, covenant integrity in your own life today?
How does this chapter encourage you to rely on the Spirit's empowerment to stand on your feet and witness faithfully in a challenging culture?

cultural_historical.title: The Exile in Babylon
cultural_historical.text: Ezekiel ministered in exile among the deportees by the River Chebar, providing a crucial priestly voice that helped preserve Israel's faith during the collapse of the monarchy and the destruction of the Temple.
